text.title: ASP.NET Core Layered Architecture
text.summary: Layer responsibilities, DI scoping rules, EF Core guidance, and CancellationToken propagation for .NET 8/9 APIs.

rule.statement: Controllers handle HTTP only: deserialize request, call one service method, return ActionResult or typed result. No business logic, no DbContext access, no direct EF queries.
rule.rationale: Controllers with domain logic cannot be unit-tested without spinning up an HTTP stack. Service isolation enables faster, cleaner tests.

rule.statement: Services accept DTOs (records), execute domain logic, call repositories or DbContext, and return DTOs. Never return EF entity objects from public service methods.
rule.rationale: Returning tracked entities from services exposes EF internals, risks circular JSON serialization, and leaks internal schema to API consumers.

rule.statement: Pass CancellationToken through every async call: ToListAsync(ct), SaveChangesAsync(ct), FindAsync(ct). Never call async EF methods without a cancellation token.
rule.rationale: Without CancellationToken, a client disconnect leaves the EF query running until completion, wasting DB connections and CPU.

rule.statement: Never inject a scoped service (DbContext, scoped repository) into a singleton. Singleton dependencies must also be singletons or transients resolved via IServiceProvider.CreateScope().
rule.rationale: A singleton holding a scoped DbContext keeps the context for the process lifetime, causing EF state corruption across concurrent requests.

rule.statement: Use C# record types with required init properties for all DTOs. Request records carry DataAnnotations or FluentValidation rules. Response records carry no logic.
rule.rationale: Records provide immutability, structural equality, and concise syntax. required init ensures all fields are set at construction without mutable setters.

rule.statement: Use keyset pagination (WHERE id > @after ORDER BY id LIMIT 20) on large tables. Never use Skip/Take on tables with >100k rows.
rule.rationale: Skip/Take (OFFSET/FETCH) scans and discards all preceding rows — performance degrades linearly with page number. Keyset pagination is O(1).

rule.statement: Inject TimeProvider and call timeProvider.GetUtcNow() instead of DateTime.UtcNow or DateTimeOffset.UtcNow.
rule.rationale: DateTime.UtcNow is a static call that cannot be controlled in tests. TimeProvider is the .NET 8 standard abstraction for testable time.
